examples.title: Torque On Door
examples.content: Push door perpendicular: Force 10 N, distance 1 m. Torque = 10×1×sin(90°) = 10 N⋅m. Perpendicular push = maximum torque!

examples.title: Magnetic Force
examples.content: Charge moving north at 10 m/s in east-pointing magnetic field 2 T. Force = Q×(v×B) points up (perpendicular to both). Use cross product.

examples.title: Angular Momentum
examples.content: Particle at position (1,0,0) with momentum (0,1,0). L = r×p gives (0,0,1). Perpendicular product measures rotation.
